evidence: Line 13: eval "$(echo "$input" | jq -r '@sh "tool_name=\(.tool_name // "") file_path=\(.tool_input.file_path // "")"' 2>/dev/null)" — eval applied to jq-processed external hook JSON input
description: eval with jq @sh output from external hook JSON; jq @sh mitigates injection risk but eval on any external data is a dangerous pattern — a jq @sh escaping bug would allow command injection via a crafted file_path value
suggested_fix: Replace eval with two separate jq -r invocations: tool_name=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""'); file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

evidence: Line 11: echo "$(date): Hook triggered with input: $input" >> /tmp/claude-hook-debug.log — writes full PostToolUse hook JSON (including tool_input with file_path and Write content) to world-readable /tmp path
description: Debug logging statement writes hook JSON payload to /tmp/claude-hook-debug.log; payload includes file content from Write hooks, exposing code and potentially credentials to any local process
suggested_fix: Remove the debug echo entirely; if logging is required, write to .claude/hook-debug.log and add that path to .gitignore

description: CURRENT_DIR sourced from jq -r '.workspace.current_dir' (line 103) and used unsanitized in git -C "$CURRENT_DIR" rev-parse; path injection via tampered status-line JSON could escape intended directory context
suggested_fix: Validate CURRENT_DIR is an absolute path with no special shell characters before passing to git -C

evidence: PEP 723 inline script metadata at lines 3-7: google-genai and pillow declared without version pins; uv run resolves latest at runtime
description: Unpinned google-genai and pillow dependencies resolved at uv run time; supply-chain change in either package could silently break or compromise execution
suggested_fix: Pin to tested ranges, e.g. google-genai>=1.16,<2 and pillow>=11,<12

evidence: Line 74: grep -qE 'eval\s+"\$|bash\s+-c\s+"\$' is a detection regex string inside a grep test — the script blocks this pattern, it does not execute it
description: Pre-scan false positive: bash-guard.sh contains eval and curl-pipe-sh patterns as grep regex strings for detection and blocking; the script is a security guard hook, not an exploit
fp_reason: The pattern appears inside a grep -qE regex argument used to DETECT and BLOCK eval usage; bash-guard.sh does not execute eval itself

evidence: Line 44: grep -qE '(curl|wget).*\|\s*(bash|sh|zsh|python|node)' is detection logic; the script blocks curl-pipe-sh, it does not execute it
description: Pre-scan false positive: curl-pipe-sh pattern is a grep detection regex inside bash-guard.sh, which actively blocks this pattern in Claude Code commands
fp_reason: Pattern appears inside grep -qE detection test in a security-guard hook that blocks the matched pattern; not an execution of curl-pipe-sh

evidence: Line 11: '! git branch --show-current' and lines 20, 27 use $(git branch --show-current) as subshell; allowed-tools lists Bash(git status), Bash(git diff), Bash(git log), Bash(git checkout), Bash(git push), Bash(gh pr create) — Bash(git branch) is absent
description: git branch --show-current is called three times in pr.md but Bash(git branch) is not declared in allowed-tools; Claude Code blocks undeclared tool calls in strict mode, causing the command to fail at the first git branch invocation
suggested_fix: Add Bash(git branch) to the allowed-tools frontmatter list

evidence: Command has 5 sequential steps (check branch/status, check diff, verify not on main, push, create PR) described in prose with 'First...', 'Let me see...', 'Now I\'ll check...' but no numbered list
description: Multi-step command lacks numbered steps; sequential flow described only in prose making step ordering ambiguous
suggested_fix: Replace prose flow description with a numbered list: 1. Check current branch and status, 2. Review changes, 3. Guard against main/master, 4. Push branch, 5. Create PR

evidence: Command runs git diff and git log against base branch but has no early exit or user message if both return empty (zero commits ahead of base)
description: No guard for zero-commits-ahead-of-base case; command would push an empty branch and open a PR with no diff
suggested_fix: After checking git log output, add: if no commits ahead of base, print an informative message and exit

evidence: allowed-tools declares Bash(git checkout) but no git checkout call appears anywhere in the command body
description: Bash(git checkout) declared in allowed-tools but never invoked; likely a copy-paste from commit.md
suggested_fix: Remove Bash(git checkout) from pr.md allowed-tools

evidence: Line 34: '[Include test plan section only if the changes warrant testing steps]' — 'warrant' is an unspecified criterion
description: Vague instruction 'if the changes warrant testing steps' gives the model no clear criterion for when to include a test plan
suggested_fix: Specify the criterion, e.g. 'Include a test plan section if any new functions, API endpoints, or UI interactions were added'

evidence: Line 32: '[Brief bullet points describing the key changes]' — 'appropriate title and description' in description line is vague
description: Vague 'appropriate title and description' — no format specification for title length, tense, or required body sections
suggested_fix: Specify title format, e.g. 'imperative-mood summary under 72 chars matching the primary commit subject'

evidence: Command runs git status and git diff but has no early-exit path when git status --porcelain returns empty; proceeds to commit steps with no changes staged
description: No handler for clean working tree; command would attempt to create commits with no changes, leading to 'nothing to commit' errors without informative user feedback
suggested_fix: Add early exit after git status: if git status --porcelain is empty, print 'Nothing to commit — working tree is clean' and exit

evidence: Line 6: 'create appropriate commit messages' and line 42: 'Stage the appropriate files for that logical unit'
description: 'appropriate' used twice without specifying criteria; the commit message style guide in the same file partially addresses this but the instruction itself remains vague
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate commit messages' with 'commit messages following the 70-char imperative-mood rules below'

evidence: Line 9: 'You SHOULD look at the full diff if needed to understand the context' — 'if needed' gives no trigger criterion
description: 'if needed' is unspecified; the model has no clear rule for when the diff lookup is required
suggested_fix: Specify condition, e.g. 'Read the full diff when commit messages are terse or fewer than 5 commits are available'

evidence: Line 27: 'The list should be suitable for a CEO or executive audience' — 'suitable' is undefined
description: 'suitable for a CEO' provides no measurable criterion; the anti-patterns section partially compensates but the instruction itself remains vague
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete rules already implied in the document: 'omit technical file names, library versions, and implementation details; use business-outcome framing'

evidence: Frontmatter has name and description but no allowed-tools field; the skill body directs execution of echo, grep, and uv run Bash commands
description: SKILL.md functions as an instruction document directing Bash command execution but declares no allowed-tools; tool scope is unbounded
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: Bash(echo:*), Bash(grep:*), Bash(uv:*) or broader Bash(*) to the frontmatter

evidence: Description field: 'for any purpose - frontend designs...' and 'Invoke this skill for ANY image generation request'
description: 'any purpose' and 'ANY image generation request' are catch-all phrasings flagged by the vague-quantifier rule (2 occurrences, -2 each)
suggested_fix: Replace 'for any purpose' with the enumerated list that follows, and replace 'ANY image generation request' with 'all image generation requests'

description: Bash(pip:*) declared in allowed-tools but no pip invocation appears in SKILL.md body; pip may be referenced in the subordinate voiceover.md but is not called from this skill file
suggested_fix: Remove Bash(pip:*) from SKILL.md allowed-tools if pip is only needed in voiceover.md; otherwise add an explicit pip usage example to the skill body

evidence: Line 96: 'Ask the user a couple quick setup questions' — 'couple' is an unspecified quantity
description: 'a couple' is vague; Phase 2 actually asks four questions (duration, theme, voice, and then 4 transition questions)
suggested_fix: Replace 'a couple quick setup questions' with 'a few setup questions (duration, theme, and voice)'

evidence: No plugin.json found in target-repo/plugins/buildatscale/, target-repo/plugins/nano-banana/, or target-repo/plugins/promo-video/ — glob for **/plugin.json returned zero results
description: No plugin.json manifest found in any plugin directory; without a manifest, claude plugin install cannot register commands or skills, and allowed-tools cannot be verified against a registry entry
suggested_fix: Add plugin.json to each plugin root declaring name, version, commands, and skills arrays

description: SKILL.md requires remotion-best-practices skill (npx skills add remotion-dev/skills) which is not bundled in the repo; unavailability of the external skill leaves Phase 3 referencing an unloaded context
suggested_fix: Document the external dependency in plugin.json as a prerequisite, or bundle a minimal local copy of the remotion-best-practices skill
